data.title: Boutique investment bank Fenchurch hires Barclays' Chawla as US co-head, sources say
data.description:
Fenchurch Advisory Partners, the boutique investment bank focused on the financial services industry, is hiring Gautam Chawla as co-head of its operations in the United States, people familiar with the matter said on Thursday.

Chawla will join the firm in November from Barclays , where he has been serving as global co-head of its financial institutions group, said the people, who spoke on condition of anonymity to discuss the move, which has not been announced publicly.

At Fenchurch, an affiliate of French investment bank Natixis (BFCEp.PA), Chawla will work alongside John Sipp, the current head of the bank's U.S. operations who joined Fenchurch when it opened its office in New York in 2022.

Fenchurch declined comment. Barclays did not respond to a request for comment.

Chawla has more than 30 years of investment banking experience, with the last five years at Barclays. Prior to that, he spent nearly three decades at Citigroup , including as co-head of global insurance investment banking, according to his LinkedIn profile.

Fenchurch was founded in London in 2003 by Malik Karim, a veteran dealmaker and former treasurer of Britain's Conservative Party. It has subsequently grown to around 70 bankers in London, Paris and New York, all focused on providing advisory services to financial companies on mergers and acquisitions, raising capital, and shareholder activism.

data.title: Ethereum Labeled ‘Wall Street Token’ as Banks Adapt to Stablecoin Demands
data.description:
Jan van Eck, CEO of investment management firm VanEck, recently described Ethereum as “the Wall Street token” while talking about its surge this quarter.

In an interview with Fox News Business this week, van Eck said that with the rise of stablecoins, every bank and financial services company now needs infrastructure to process them.Ethereum’s Wall Street Moment

van Eck explained that if one person wants to send stablecoins, the recipient’s bank must either handle that transaction directly or rely on another institution to do so. According to van Eck, the real winners in this transition will be the blockchains that provide the foundation for these transactions.

He believes Ethereum, or other networks built on its Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM) methodology, will be central to driving this new financial architecture.

“If I want to send you stablecoins, your bank has to figure it out, or you find some other institution to do that. The winner is, who’s going to be building on these blockchains? It’s going to be Ethereum or something that uses Ethereum’s methodology, which is called EVM.”

The regulatory landscape for stablecoins has witnessed a tremendous change with the passage of the Guiding and Establishing National Innovation for US Stablecoins Act (GENIUS Act), which was signed into law on July 18th this year.

As the first federal legislation of its kind, the act provides a framework to ensure stablecoins are transparent, fully backed, and safely integrated into the US financial system.Post-Genius

The market’s reaction to GENIUS was swift. CryptoQuant recently reported that Binance’s stablecoin reserves surged from $32 billion to $36 billion shortly after the law’s approval.

Institutions are also accelerating their push into this sector. Stripe, for one, supports stablecoin payouts in over 100 countries and is developing its own Layer 1 blockchain to control payment rails. Circle, fresh off a successful IPO, is expanding beyond issuance with its Circle Payment Network (CPN) and a proprietary Layer 1 where USDC will be the native asset.

Even traditional giants are adapting – Visa recently introduced stablecoin settlement APIs to support round-the-clock global payments. Its rival, Mastercard,teamedup with OKX and Nuvei earlier this year to support global stablecoin payments, letting users spend from wallets and merchants accept USDC.

data.title: Treasury Yields Mixed Ahead of July PCE — Market Talk
data.description:
1546 ET - Treasury yields are mixed ahead of July PCE inflation and as the battle for control of the Fed escalates. Governor Cook sues Trump to block his move to fire her, which has put the institution's independence in question. Some economists say the threat from the administration could be an upward driver for long-term Treasury yields. Tomorrow, headline PCE inflation is expected to slow down and core to accelerate, both slightly, in a WSJ survey. The 10-year declines 0.031 percentage point to 4.206% and the two-year rises 0.010 p.p. to 3.634%. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1513 ET - Live cattle futures take another step away from recent highs, while hogs extend their recovery, as the end of the summer season approaches. The USDA reports net export sales of beef last week were up 34% from the previous period and 40% above the four-week average. Pork did even better, with weekly exports up 53% from the four-week average. CBOT live cattle contract for October closes off 1%, at $2.3693 a pound, while lean hogs rise 0.4% to 94.275 cents a pound. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1448 ET - The number of replacement heifers available to enter the dairy herd as milk producing cows has fallen to a 20-year low, and replacements could fall even further, according to CoBank. A report from CoBank's Knowledge Exchange says the national dairy heifer shortage could persist and grow deeper in the next two years, which would limit growth in the milk supply, before a recovery begins in 2027. At the same time, CoBank said, the U.S. is experiencing an historic $10 billion investment in new dairy processing facilities expected to come online through 2027. According to CoBank, the incentive to produce calves destined for the beef supply stems from a contraction in the U.S. cattle supply, which currently stands at a 75-year low, and which has helped drive beef prices to record highs. CoBank is a cooperative bank serving industries across rural America. (stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com)

1444 ET - Kohl's second-quarter results are further evidence the department-store chain is losing share to other retail channels, including off-price retailers, UBS analysts say in a note. While the results were better than expected, Kohl's same-store sales declined for the fifteenth consecutive quarter, and with the company in the early stages of a turnaround plan, there likely won't be any meaningful improvement until at least 2026, the analysts say. They expect Wall Street's earnings per-share estimates to move lower over the next year given weak sales, tariff-related cost increases and an increase in promotional offers during the coming holiday season compared to the prior year, keeping sentiment muted. Shares fall 6.1% to $15.19. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1421 ET - Cooper Cos.'s solid margins and improving free cash flow should help the company's stock despite the company's poor third-quarter earnings, say Stifel analysts, who label the stock as a "buy." The company's adjusted operating margin of 26.1% beat Wall Street estimates and Cooper said operating efficiencies are expected to continue through a restructuring in 2026. Free cash flow also beat expectations by a wide margin with working-capital efficiencies expected to continue bolstering the company's cash in coming years. The stock is down 11%, to $66.27, Thursday. That decline gives investors an opportunity to buy, the analysts say. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

1416 ET - Jack Daniel's Tennessee Whiskey maker Brown-Forman sees weakness in the U.S. market for spirits as slightly more cyclical than structural, CEO Lawson Whiting says on a call with analysts. Inflation and high interest rates are still hurting customers' ability to spend, and tariffs are putting pressure on them as well, Whiting says. Structural considerations-like the rising popularity of cannabis, more use of GLP-1 drugs and a general wellness push among consumers--wouldn't be able to tank the alcohol market as quickly as has been seen, the CEO says. "There is some continuum between cyclical and structural," he says. "But I still feel like the cyclical side is a little bit bigger piece of it." Shares retreat 5.1% to $28.89. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1358 ET [Dow Jones]--Grain futures stage a late session recovery, albeit remaining close to recent lows, as markets face robust crops while global demand looks unable to keep up. The jury is still out on how healthy U.S. corn and soybean crops will be, but few doubt production will be massive. American farmers have the added worry of decreasing Chinese demand due to trade tensions while a deal remains elusive. On the CBOT, corn and wheat rise nearly 1% while soybeans are flat after spending most of the day in the red. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com)

1329 ET - Five Below shoppers are responding well to the discount retailer's price increases, UBS analyst Michael Lasser says. Normally, customers would gradually start buying or visiting less after a retailer makes mass price changes, Lasser says. But Five Below has actually received more visits and spending over the past few months since it initiated price hikes in April and May in response to tariffs. Lasser thinks that's because customers are recognizing the widening price gaps between Five Below and other retailers, as Five Below still hasn't passed the full cost of tariffs down to sticker prices. That leaves Five Below with room to potentially raise prices further. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

1313 ET - Higher-than-expected export sales aren't enough to dispel oversupply concerns and soybeans futures extend losses. "Good growing conditions continue in the Midwest with cool and mostly dry weather in the forecast," Price Group's Jack Scoville writes. Moreover, China hasn't been buying much U.S. soybeans and "traders are worried that demand for the new crop will be a lot less this year," Scoville says. "Prices are still higher in Brazil, but China and other buyers are still buying there for political reasons." Soybeans fall 0.7% on the CBOT. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1300 ET - Pure Storage's strong 2Q report reflects widespread growth across categories, but it's tough to find an exact catalyst for the results, Wedbush Securities analysts say in a note. "Our only conundrum is we can't point to any exact factors that are catalyzing PSTG's outperformance now specifically," the analysts say, noting competitor NetApp's relatively disappointing results suggest any pickup in storage sales likely isn't broad based. Nevertheless, with better trends across the board, the analysts raise their price target on Pure Storage's stock to $80 from $70. Shares rise 31% to $79.62. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1249 ET - Industry brand loyalty rates declined slightly in the first half of 2025 following last year's rebound, according to a S&P Global Mobility analysis of new vehicle registration data June. The industry's brand loyalty rate through June stood at 51.1%, down 1.4 percentage points from the same period in 2024. The decrease marks a reversal from 2024's year-over-year gain, showing continued volatility in loyalty trends as the market continues to stabilize post-pandemic. Both mainstream and luxury brands contributed to the decline. The number of households returning to market rose 4.2% year-over-year, representing the third consecutive year of improvement. (chris.wack@wsj.com)

1235 ET - Pure Storage continued to make progress on its engagement with Meta and other hyperscaler customers in its latest quarter, setting the stage for a key long-term growth driver, J.P. Morgan analysts say in a note. Pure Storage management noted Meta initiated its first volume deployments, resulting in the first revenue contributions from the collaboration, and the company is looking to enable its other hyperscaler customers to replace raw storage with its DirectFlash Modules. The analysts say revenue contributions from such engagements will be minimal this fiscal year, but will be a key component of expectations for further accelerating growth in the future. They raise their price target on the stock to $80 from $70. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

data.title: Kohl's Is Maintained at Underweight by JP Morgan
Here's one way investors can protect their portfolios as Trump keeps pressure on the Fed
data.description:
By Vivien Lou Chen

Treasury inflation-protected securities may be a better option for investors than bond-market steepener trades, says strategist Mehmet Beceren of Rosenberg Research

Workers hang an image of President Donald Trump along the side of the U.S. Labor Department's headquarters building in Washington as Trump's battle against the Federal Reserve heats up.

Rising political pressure from the Trump administration on the independent Federal Reserve has produced a widening gap between long- and short-term Treasury yields over most of August - and there is more than one way for traders to position for a continuation of this pattern.

Typically, whenever there is an expectation that long-term yields will rise at a faster pace relative to shorter-term rates, traders rely on what's known as the "steepener trade." This trading strategy is designed to profit from a widening difference between long-term rates, like the 10-year Treasury yield BX:TMUBMUSD10Y, and shorter-term yields, such as the 2-year rate BX:TMUBMUSD02Y. As of the end of Wednesday's trading session, the 2s/10s spread had widened to 61.4 basis points, up from roughly 40 basis point in late July. It was 57 basis points on Thursday.

Toronto-based Rosenberg Research, founded by economist and former Wall Street strategist David Rosenberg, sees a better approach than the steepener trade for investors looking to position for continued pressure from Trump on the Fed. The approach favors Treasury inflation-protected securities, which have been largely unloved by investors in recent years.

One of the biggest reasons cited by Rosenberg Research in favor of TIPS is the need for investors to hedge both policy uncertainty and inflation risk. According to the firm, an important development taking place right now under the Trump administration is the shift toward a pro-growth stance with a tolerance for higher inflation.

TIPS were created to help investors manage risks around changes in the cost of living, and are sold by the U.S. Treasury for terms of five, 10 or 30 years. These securities have a principal value that is adjusted to keep pace with the consumer-price index, and an interest rate that remains fixed. Because the interest being paid is based on adjusted principal, the amount of interest payments can vary. TIPS can also be sold before they mature.

"Markets are adjusting to the erosion of central-bank independence," said Mehmet Beceren, a strategist at Rosenberg Research, in a note released on Thursday. "Monetary policy is on track to become a tool of nationalist industrial strategy, aligned with broader political objectives, rather than anchored in technocratic discipline."

The strategist added: "We do not think a tactical 2s-10s 'steepener' trade via bond futures is a good way to benefit from rising curve-steepening risks." For one thing, "a long-short position has a high cost of carry and only pays off with high leverage," he noted. "If long-term inflation expectations become another layer of policy uncertainty, we think TIPS are a better option to replace outright duration in portfolios."

Trump's blunt attacks on the Fed have led to rising uncertainty about the independence of the central bank that has the potential to produce higher volatility through year-end. Meanwhile, the Fed seems to have committed itself to a rate-cutting cycle, which should put a lid on short-term yields and reinforce the prospects for a steepening trading dynamic.

On Thursday, financial markets appeared relatively steady even after Fed governor Lisa Cook sued Trump for threatening to fire her over alleged mortgage fraud. The president has also been critical toward Fed Chair Jerome Powell for failing to cut interest rates fast enough.

Read: Lisa Cook's Trump lawsuit opens new chapter in showdown over Fed independence

All three major stock indexes DJIA SPX COMP were moving higher in afternoon trading, Treasury yields were mixed, and market-based measures of future inflation were little changed. The next major U.S. inflation update lands on Friday in the form of the personal-consumption expenditures index for July.

-Vivien Lou Chen

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: US FTC chair alleges Gmail uses 'partisan filtering'
data.description:
U.S. Federal Trade Commission Chairman Andrew Ferguson alleged that Gmail uses what the FTC calls "partisan filtering" and raised the issue in a letter to the CEO of Google parent company Alphabet's , Sundar Pichai, the regulator said on Thursday.

"My understanding from recent reporting is that Gmail's spam filters routinely block messages from reaching consumers when those messages come from Republican senders but fail to block similar messages sent by Democrats," the letter released by the FTC claimed.

Google has denied similar claims from Republicans and conservatives in the past.

data.title: Abercrombie & Fitch Is Maintained at Overweight by JP Morgan
Vittoria Lowers Size of Planned IPO to 1.8M Shares From 2.25M
FIVE BELOW, INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Five Below, Inc., a leading high-growth value retailer offering trend-right, high-quality products loved by tweens, teens, and beyond, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of fiscal 2025. The report highlights significant financial growth and operational achievements, underscoring the company's robust performance and strategic expansion efforts.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Sales: $1,026.8 million, increased by 23.7% from the previous year, driven by both non-comparable and comparable sales growth.
  • Gross Profit: $342.4 million, representing a 26.0% increase, with a gross margin improvement to 33.3% due to decreased store occupancy costs as a percentage of net sales.
  • Operating Income: $52.4 million, reflecting a 26.3% increase, supported by higher sales and improved gross margin.
  • Net Income: $42.8 million, up by 29.6% from the previous year, attributed to increased sales and improved operational efficiency.
  • Diluted Income Per Common Share: $0.77, compared to $0.60 in the previous year, indicating enhanced profitability per share.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company reported a breakdown of revenue by product lines, with Leisure accounting for 45.8% of net sales, Fashion and Home at 30.1%, and Snack and Seasonal at 24.1% for the thirteen weeks ended August 2, 2025.
  • Sales Units: The company operated 1,858 stores as of August 2, 2025, an increase from 1,667 stores as of August 3, 2024, reflecting the company's expansion strategy.
  • Comparable Sales Performance: Comparable sales increased by 12.4% for the thirteen weeks ended August 2, 2025, driven by an 8.7% increase in the number of transactions and a 3.4% increase in the average transaction value.
  • New Store Openings: The company opened new stores in fiscal 2025, contributing to a non-comparable sales increase of $98.4 million for the thirteen weeks ended August 2, 2025.
  • Operational Efficiency: Gross margin improved to 33.3% for the thirteen weeks ended August 2, 2025, up from 32.7% in the prior year period, primarily due to a decrease in store occupancy costs as a percentage of net sales.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to open approximately 150 net new stores in fiscal 2025, with a focus on expanding its store footprint and enhancing its e-commerce capabilities.
  • Store Expansion Strategy: The company has entered into 85 new retail leases with average terms of approximately 10 years, indicating a commitment to long-term growth and expansion.

SEC Filing: FIVE BELOW, INC [ FIVE ] - 10-Q - Aug. 28, 2025

data.title: New Tariffs on Low-Cost Imports Kick In on Friday — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Anita Hamilton

Americans are about to feel a fresh blow from new levies on imports. Starting Friday, items valued at $800 or less that are shipped directly to consumers from abroad will no longer be exempted from tariffs. Some countries, including Mexico, Japan, Taiwan, and Australia, have already stopped such shipments in anticipation of the rule change.

For packages that do make it to the U.S., tariffs will be either the same rate imposed on the country of origin or a flat fee of $80 to $200, also depending on origin. Personal gifts valued at $100 or less will remain duty free as will $200 in personal items that U.S. travelers bring back with them.

The rule change comes amid a steep increase in the number of low-value imports shipped directly to consumers. Over the past 10 years, these so-called de minimis shipments have risen from 139 million to 1.36 billion, according to U.S. Customs and Border Protection. Those tariff-free shipments were valued at an estimated $64.6 billion in 2024 and accounted for 92% of all cargo entering the country.

A rule change in 2016 that raised the value of goods exempted from tariffs from $200 to $800 helped drive the increase in shipments as online shopping took off. Retailers such as Temu and Shein along with Etsy vendors located outside the U.S. were able to capitalize on the loophole to grow sales.

Because many de minimis shipments previously came from China, the Trump administration suspended the exemption for Chinese goods back in May. The move was also intended to stop the flow of synthetic opioids into the U.S. from there.

Part of the reason why countries have pre-emptively stopped shipments to the U.S. is to give them time to work out the payment process for the goods. Some international couriers have halted commercial shipments to the U.S. over similar concerns.

Write to Anita Hamilton at anita.hamilton@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Why Broadcom (AVGO) Stock Is Climbing Today
data.description:
Aug 28 - An upbeat forecast for artificial-intelligence spending from Nvidia set the tone for the chip sector, lifting Broadcom shares after the company reported results. While Nvidia framed AI demand as a continuing investment driver, the firm also flagged signs of slower revenue expansion, a mix that left peers reacting unevenly.

Broadcom saw its stock rise more than 3% on Thursday as traders weighed how stronger AI budgets could boost demand for networking and infrastructure chips. At the same time, market participants noted that not all chipmakers will benefit equally; customers prioritize specific components and projects, and spending cycles vary across data-center and consumer markets.

Sector-wide responses remained mixed because an upbeat AI outlook does not erase near-term growth headwinds. Companies with heavy exposure to legacy businesses face different pressures than those tied closely to AI-related cloud infrastructure. For investors, the key question becomes which firms can convert AI spending into sustained revenue gains amid economic uncertainty.

Analysts called the results a useful signal rather than a definitive shift and said company-level guidance and customer pipeline details will determine winners and losers in the months ahead.

data.title: Onyxcoin to Release Smart Wallet v.1.0.3
data.description:
Onyxcoin has launched version 1.0.3 of its Smart Wallet, now available on both Google Play Store and Apple App Store. The update aims to improve the user experience, especially when managing XCN and other digital assets.

XCN Info

Onyxcoin (XCN), formerly known as Chain, is a web3 blockchain platform founded in 2014 to build reliable financial services. It combines scalability with user-friendly smart contracts, supports multi-asset operations, and enables cross-network interoperability. The core mission of Onyxcoin is to address inefficiencies in the traditional financial system by providing a unified distributed ledger for transparent and secure settlements.

The protocol is built on a cloud-based architecture with APIs for regulated ledgers, offering access to RPC and WebSocket endpoints for network interaction. It applies the principle of least authority, ensuring users retain control over assets regardless of ledger synchronization. Additionally, users can run their own network nodes to support the platform. Governance is managed through the Onyx DAO, where decisions are made via on-chain voting, and audited smart contracts ensure security and transparency.

XCN is the platform’s native token, used for paying for services and products, staking, and participating in governance through the Onyx DAO. Holders of XCN can propose and vote on network upgrades and earn rewards through staking. The token functions both as a utility for transactions and as a governance mechanism for the decentralized ecosystem.

data.description:

FLOKI

Drop your questions in the form. Each selectedâ€¦ pic.twitter.com/usrpr7n3G4

Aug 28, 2025

FLOKI Info

FLOKI is a meme cryptocurrency, much like Dogecoin and Shiba Inu. It was created in 2021 and named after the dog of Elon Musk, the CEO of Tesla and SpaceX, who has a significant influence over cryptocurrency markets. The token is part of a broader ecosystem that includes an NFT marketplace and a game. As with all meme tokens, FLOKI's value is largely driven by community sentiment and social media trends. Investors should exercise caution and thoroughly research before investing in these types of digital assets, as they can be highly volatile.

data.title: Phillips 66 to Begin Idling Units at Los Angeles Refinery Next Week — OPIS
data.description:
Phillips 66 will begin to idle certain units next week at its 147,000 b/d Los Angeles refinery in preparation of its planned closure by the end of the year, a source familiar with the situation said Thursday.

The refinery will continue to produce after next week, the source said, without providing details about the level of output.

"(The) process of idling the facilities requires a complex and multi-phased approach. The refinery units will begin idling by Q4 2025 as planned," a company spokesman said in an email statement.

"We continue to focus on maintaining safe, reliable, and environmentally responsible operations as we work to begin idling facilities," the spokesman said. "Our timeline around idling has not changed."

In Phillips 66's second-quarter earnings conference call in late July, Chief Executive Lashier said the company remained on course to shut its Los Angeles refinery by the end of 2025. The downstream company first announced the planned closure of the refinery in October 2024.

Lashier previously said the closure of the facility, the company's last refinery in California, will create an opportunity to import gasoline into the state. Lashier also said Phillips 66 intends to make up the lost supply through importing production from its 110,500 b/d Ferndale, Wash., refinery.

Valero Energy in July also said it was proceeding with plans to close its 149,000 b/d Benicia refinery in Northern California by next spring.

The shutdown of the two refineries would cut in-state refining capacity by 17%, according to the Energy Information Administration.

Earlier this year, California Gov. Gavin Newsom asked the California Energy Commission to review the state's refining capacity in the wake of Valero and Phillips 66 announcements. CEC previously said it would support a stable and affordable fuel supply in the state by ensuring a resilient supply chain and adequate product inventories.

The Los Angeles refinery includes crude distillation, naphtha reforming, fluid catalytic cracking, alkylation, hydrocracking, and delayed coking units and it produces a high percentage of transportation fuels. The facility produces CARB gasoline and other products that are supplied to California, Nevada and Arizona.

This content was created by Oil Price Information Service, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. OPIS is run independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

  • Reporting by Frank Tang, ftang@opisnet.com; Editing by Bayan Raji, braji@opisnet.com

data.title: Axie Infinity to Launch Classic Competitive Season 11
data.description:
Axie Infinity has kicked off Classic Competitive Season 11, running from August 28 to September 18. Players can now enter the arena to compete for over 35,000 AXS in total rewards. Both Battle and Premium Passes are available. The season can be accessed via hub.skymavis.com.

Refer to the official tweet by AXS:

Axie Infinity
@AxieInfinity

Axie Classic Competitive Season 11 is LIVE!

Enter the arena now âš”ï¸

🗓ï¸Â Aug 28th to Sept 18th

🏆Â Over 35K AXS in total rewards

🎟ï¸Â Battle and Premium Passes are LIVE

Play Classic 👇

🔗Â : https://t.co/0w37ht00lX

Full announcement 👇

📜Â : https://t.co/5tqO4YK3oD pic.twitter.com/Ognn67dqzY

Aug 28, 2025

AXS Info

Axie Infinity is a blockchain game developed by Sky Mavis. The game runs on Ethereum and utilizes non-fungible tokens (NFTs) that represent unique items or characters.

At the core of the game are creatures called "Axies," which players can collect, breed, use for battles, and even use for virtual land farming. Each Axie has different attributes and statistics, and players can use their Axies in player-versus-player (PvP) battles or player-versus-environment (PvE) adventures.

One of the unique aspects of Axie Infinity is that players can earn digital assets and cryptocurrency while playing the game. This makes Axie Infinity an example of a "play-to-earn" game, where in-game actions can lead to real financial benefits.

The AXS token (Axie Infinity Shards) is the native token of Axie Infinity and is used for game governance, participating in voting for game rules, earning rewards, and more.

data.title: Mobox to Launch Dragonverse Season 22
data.description:
MOBOX has launched Season 22 of its Dragonverse event, running from August 28 to September 7 at 08:00 UTC. The prize pool includes 6,000 MBOX, 10 million eMDBL, and 50% of all in-game purchases. Additional item drops are available for MOBOX holders, and the top five $MBOX spenders will receive exclusive bonus rewards.

Refer to the official tweet by MBOX:

MOBOX
@MOBOX_Official

The path has revealed itself. 🌿 Welcome to Season 22.

Your window is open now

â³Duration: Aug 28 - Sep 7th, 8 AM UTC

Hereâ€™s the treasure waiting at the finish line:

🌸 Prize Pool: 6,000 $MBOX + 10,000,000 eMDBL + 50% of all purchases!

Keep your eyes peeled for theseâ€¦ pic.twitter.com/CjpKkloHBM

Aug 28, 2025

MBOX Info

MOBOX is a gaming platform that integrates yield farming with NFT farming to establish a free-to-play and play-to-earn ecosystem. It envisions connecting various metaverses through NFTs and aims for cross-game and cross-platform NFT interoperability. The platform focuses on enhancing the utility of NFTs in gaming and developing real-time applications for these digital assets.

MOBOX includes several key components:

A decentralized NFT marketplace for trading, renting, and lending MOMO NFTs.

A game creator featuring APIs for developers to launch and publish games using MOBOX tokens and MOMO NFTs.

An NFT creator allowing users to craft their own MOMO NFTs based on the ERC721 standard.

The MOMOverse, a cross-platform metaverse rewarding engagement and creation.

Decentralized Autonomous Guilds (DAG) for community building and resource sharing.

NFT interoperability enabling asset usage across the ecosystem and in multiple games.

MOBOX chain, an NFT POS blockchain network controlled by in-game assets, promoting carbon-neutral and efficient transactions.

MBOX serves as the native utility token in the MOBOX ecosystem. Functions as in-game currency for purchases, character acquisitions, and incentivizes player activities like PvP battles. Enables governance, allowing holders to submit and vote on proposals influencing the platform’s development. Used in liquidity mining, with staking in various pools or staking MOMO NFTs to earn rewards. Can be staked for a chance to obtain MOMO NFT Mystery Boxes.
